content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic rewards (from autonomous agent research) can be repurposed as a active selection signal for scalable, long-term agent memory, where the agent prioritizes retaining unexpected, information-dense episodic memories that trigger intrinsic curiosity instead of just retaining high-reward outcomes for task performance. This creates a self-sustaining cycle where the agent’s inherent curiosity grows its useful memory repository without constant human labeling of what to store.
